releases.name: GPT-5.6 Sol
releases.description: OpenAI's flagship GPT-5.6 family model for complex work across coding, research, science, cybersecurity, computer use, and design. It is notable as the smartest GPT-5.6 tier exposed in ChatGPT release notes for paid ChatGPT users.

releases.name: Muse Spark 1.1
releases.description: Meta Superintelligence Labs' multimodal reasoning model for agentic tasks. It is notable for major gains in tool use, computer use, coding, and multimodal understanding and for debuting in the public preview Meta Model API.

releases.name: Grok 4.5
releases.description: xAI's frontier model for coding, agentic tasks, and knowledge work. It is notable for configurable reasoning effort and API availability as the flagship Grok model for code and tool-calling use cases.

releases.name: Leanstral 1.5
releases.description: Mistral's Apache-2.0 model for Lean 4 formal proof engineering and code verification. It is notable for strong formal-methods benchmarks, practical repository verification, Hugging Face weights, and a free API endpoint.

releases.name: Claude Sonnet 5
releases.description: Anthropic's most agentic Sonnet model, aimed at coding, tool use, and everyday work at Sonnet speed and price. It is notable for bringing Opus-class agentic coding capability into the Sonnet tier.

releases.name: AFM 3 Core Advanced
releases.description: Apple's most powerful on-device Apple Foundation Model, a sparse 20B-parameter model optimized for capable Apple silicon. It is notable as a natively multimodal on-device model supporting features like expressive voices and higher-accuracy dictation.

releases.name: ADM 3 Cloud (Image)
releases.description: Apple's server-based image generation and editing model running on Private Cloud Compute. It is notable for powering advanced photo editing tools and the new Image Playground while preserving Apple's private-cloud privacy guarantees.

releases.name: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra 550B-A55B
releases.description: NVIDIA's 550B-total, 55B-active frontier-scale Nemotron model for complex agents, long-context analysis, code, math, and science. It is notable for open weights, training data, recipes, and commercial/non-commercial availability under OpenMDW-1.1.

releases.name: MAI-Thinking-1
releases.description: Microsoft AI's first reasoning model, trained from scratch on enterprise-grade, commercially licensed data. It is notable as a 35B-active-parameter model with a 256K context window for complex multi-step instructions, long-context reasoning, and code generation.

releases.name: MAI-Image-2.5
releases.description: Microsoft's image model family for text-to-image and image-to-image workloads. It is notable as Microsoft's first model serving both generation and editing workloads across PowerPoint, OneDrive, and Foundry.

releases.name: MAI-Code-1
releases.description: Microsoft's inference-efficient coding model tuned for GitHub. It is notable for availability in Copilot and VS Code as part of Microsoft's expanded MAI model family.

releases.name: NVIDIA Cosmos 3
releases.description: NVIDIA's open frontier foundation model for physical AI, combining physical reasoning, world generation, and action generation. It is notable for unifying VLM reasoning and diffusion-based generation for robots, autonomous vehicles, and physical-world simulation.

releases.name: Stable Audio 3.0
releases.description: Stability AI's fully licensed generative-audio model family for music and sound effects. It is notable for open-weight Small SFX, Small, and Medium models, variable-length generation up to more than six minutes, and a Large API/enterprise tier.

releases.name: Gemini 3.5 Flash
releases.description: Google DeepMind's model-card index listed Gemini 3.5 Flash as updated in May 2026. It is notable as a Flash-tier Gemini model in the 3.5 generation.

releases.name: Grok Build 0.1
releases.description: xAI's coding model trained specifically for agentic coding workflows. It is notable as the model slug behind the Grok Build early-access coding agent experience.

releases.name: Mistral Medium 3.5
releases.description: Mistral's frontier-class multimodal model optimized for agentic and coding use cases. It is notable because Mistral released it as open weights under a Modified MIT license.

releases.name: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano Omni
releases.description: NVIDIA's open multimodal model for video, audio, image, and text reasoning in agentic workflows. It is notable for combining vision and audio encoders in a 30B-A3B hybrid MoE architecture with open weights, datasets, and training techniques.

releases.name: DeepSeek-V4-Pro
releases.description: DeepSeek's V4 Preview flagship model with 1.6T total and 49B active parameters. It is notable for open weights, 1M context, thinking/non-thinking modes, and performance positioned against top closed-source models.

releases.name: DeepSeek-V4-Flash
releases.description: DeepSeek's efficient V4 Preview model with 284B total and 13B active parameters. It is notable for open weights, 1M context, lower-cost API use, and reasoning capabilities close to V4-Pro.

releases.name: Qwen3.6-27B
releases.description: Alibaba Qwen's dense 27B open-source multimodal model for agentic coding. It is notable for flagship-level coding performance, text and multimodal reasoning, and open weights on Hugging Face and ModelScope.

releases.name: GPT Image 2
releases.description: OpenAI's state-of-the-art image generation and editing model for high-quality image outputs with flexible sizes and high-fidelity image inputs. It is notable as the API model behind ChatGPT Images 2.0 and image editing/generation workflows.

releases.name: Claude Opus 4.7
releases.description: Anthropic's latest Opus model with improvements in advanced software engineering and difficult long-running tasks. It is also notable for better vision resolution and higher-quality professional artifacts like interfaces, slides, and docs.

releases.name: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B
releases.description: Alibaba Qwen's sparse 35B-total, 3B-active MoE model for agentic coding. It is notable for open weights on Hugging Face and ModelScope, multimodal thinking/non-thinking modes, and API availability as qwen3.6-flash.

releases.name: Grok 4.20
releases.description: xAI's Grok 4.20 model card described it as a model with advanced reasoning and multi-agent capabilities. It supported single-agent and multi-agent modes and multimodal reasoning over text and images.

releases.name: Mistral Small 4
releases.description: Mistral's hybrid open model unifying reasoning, multimodal, and agentic coding capabilities in one system. It is notable for Apache 2.0 licensing, text and image inputs, and day-zero availability through Mistral, Hugging Face, and NVIDIA NIM.

releases.name: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Super
releases.description: NVIDIA's open hybrid Mamba-Transformer MoE model for agentic reasoning. It is notable for a 1M-token context window, 120B total and 12B active parameters, and open weights, datasets, and recipes.

releases.name: GPT-5.4 Thinking
releases.description: Frontier OpenAI model combining reasoning, coding, and agentic workflows. The release is notable for stronger coding, tool use, document work, and longer thinking-context management in ChatGPT.

releases.name: Gemini 3.1 Pro
releases.description: Google DeepMind's Gemini 3.1 Pro model card was published or updated in February 2026 as part of the Gemini 3 family. It is notable as a Pro-tier Gemini model in the post-Gemini-3 lineup.

releases.name: Claude Sonnet 4.6
releases.description: Anthropic's Sonnet 4.6 delivered frontier performance across coding, agents, and professional work at scale. It was framed as able to compress multi-day coding projects into hours and produce production-ready solutions.

releases.name: Qwen3.5-397B-A17B
releases.description: Alibaba Qwen's first Qwen3.5 open-weight model, a 397B-total, 17B-active native vision-language MoE. It is notable for reasoning, coding, agent, and multimodal benchmark performance and for open-weight community availability.

releases.name: Gemini 3 Deep Think
releases.description: Google's specialized Gemini 3 reasoning mode for science, research, and engineering challenges. It was notable for extending Deep Think availability to the Gemini app for Ultra subscribers and early API access for select researchers, engineers, and enterprises.

releases.name: GPT-5.3-Codex
releases.description: OpenAI's agentic coding model that combined Codex and GPT-5 training stacks. It was positioned as a step-change from code generation toward general-purpose coding-agent work.

releases.name: FLUX.2 [klein]
releases.description: Black Forest Labs' fastest FLUX image-model family for unified generation and editing. It is notable for sub-second real-time image workflows, consumer-hardware deployment, open weights, and Apache 2.0 licensing for the 4B variants.

releases.name: LTX-2
releases.description: Lightricks' DiT-based audio-video foundation model for synchronized audio and video generation. It is notable for bundling modern video-generation capabilities into one open-access model with downloadable assets and local inference tooling.

releases.name: GLM-4.7
releases.description: Z.ai's coding-focused GLM model with stronger programming, agent, and multi-step reasoning capabilities. It is notable for public weights on Hugging Face and ModelScope plus availability through Z.ai API and developer coding agents.

releases.name: GPT Image 1.5
releases.description: OpenAI's late-2025 flagship image model powering the new ChatGPT Images experience and API access as GPT Image 1.5. It improved precise editing, detail preservation, dense text rendering, and generation speed over earlier ChatGPT Images.

releases.name: GPT-5.2
releases.description: OpenAI's GPT-5.2 series was described as its most capable model series for professional knowledge work. It was notable for stronger performance on spreadsheets, presentations, code, image perception, long context, tools, and multi-step projects.

releases.name: Mistral Large 3
releases.description: Mistral's 675B-parameter total, 41B-active mixture-of-experts flagship in the Mistral 3 family. It is notable as a permissively licensed Apache 2.0 open-weight frontier model with image understanding.

releases.name: Ministral 3
releases.description: Mistral's small-model line in 3B, 8B, and 14B sizes with base, instruct, and reasoning variants. It is notable for Apache 2.0 licensing and native multimodal capabilities targeted at edge and local deployment.

releases.name: Amazon Nova 2 Lite
releases.description: Amazon's fast, cost-effective Nova 2 reasoning model for everyday workloads. It is notable for extended thinking, built-in tools, remote MCP support, multimodal inputs, and a one-million-token context window in Bedrock.

releases.name: Amazon Nova 2 Pro (Preview)
releases.description: Amazon's preview Nova 2 model for highly complex multi-step tasks. It is notable for advanced agentic tasks such as multi-document analysis, video reasoning, and software migration, with early access for Nova Forge customers.

releases.name: Amazon Nova 2 Sonic
releases.description: Amazon's speech-to-speech foundation model for real-time conversational AI. It is notable for unified speech understanding and generation, multilingual voice support, cross-modal interaction, and Bedrock availability.

releases.name: DeepSeek-V3.2
releases.description: DeepSeek's reasoning-first model built for agents and tool use. It is notable as the official successor to V3.2-Exp, with availability on app, web, API, and open-source model releases on Hugging Face.

releases.name: Gemini 3 Pro
releases.description: Google's Gemini 3 Pro was introduced as its most intelligent model and strongest multimodal understanding model at launch. It was notable for day-one availability across Search AI Mode, Gemini app, AI Studio, Vertex AI, Antigravity, and Gemini CLI.
